title: Driver inline notes field on My Jobs view
description: Allow drivers to write freeform notes or deviation info on their assigned jobs, directly inline in the My Jobs view. Notes are saved via a new dedicated PATCH /api/jobs/:id/notes endpoint. Dispatcher and Admin can see driver notes in the Jobs view (read-only).
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/jobs/:id/notes accepts { driverNotes: string } (max 1000 chars, may be empty string to clear). Requires authentication. Only the driver assigned to the job may call this endpoint — return 403 for any other user. Returns updated job as { data: job }.

MyJobsPage renders a driverNotes textarea inline below each job row. The textarea shows current driverNotes value (or empty). An auto-save or explicit Save button triggers PATCH /api/jobs/:id/notes. Show a brief 'Saved' confirmation on success and an error message on failure.
Driver notes textarea is only editable when job status is ASSIGNED or IN_PROGRESS. For COMPLETED jobs the field is shown read-only.
JobsPage (dispatcher/admin view) shows driverNotes as read-only text below the job row if driverNotes is non-empty. Label it 'Driver notes:'.
GET /api/jobs and GET /api/jobs/my responses include driverNotes field.
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors after implementation.
notes: Use a separate endpoint /api/jobs/:id/notes instead of the existing /api/jobs/:id/status to keep concerns separate and avoid accidental overwrites. The 403 check must verify req.user.id === job.assignedDriverId at the service/route level — not just by role. For auto-save UX, debounce is acceptable but a simple Save button is fine too. Do not expose driverNotes in any admin edit modal — it is driver-owned data.
